mnemonics: FOIL for Functions: The output of the First function is the Input to the Outer function to get the Last result.
Inverse Function Switch: An inverse function 'undoes' the original, so just swap the x and y and solve for the new y.
keyPoints: Function composition (f ∘ g)(x) means f(g(x)) - apply g first, then apply f to the result.
A function must be one-to-one (injective) to have an inverse, meaning each output comes from exactly one input.
The composition of a function and its inverse yields the identity function: f(f⁻¹(x)) = x and f⁻¹(f(x)) = x.
commonMistakes: Assuming function composition is commutative: (f ∘ g)(x) is not necessarily equal to (g ∘ f)(x).
Confusing the notation f⁻¹(x) with the reciprocal 1/f(x); they are entirely different concepts.
Assuming every function has an inverse; only bijective functions (one-to-one and onto) have inverses.
